# ancir-mcp

> Lets LLMs/agents build an AnCiR chronobiology session (data + analyses + plots) as a
> session.json that opens in the GUI. Two paths: (A) STATIC EMISSION — the model emits one
> JSON draft, a pure normalizer (src/emit) turns it into a session, and the AnCiR SPA computes
> it in the browser; needs no engine, so it ships as a Cloudflare Worker (worker/). (B) MCP —
> AnCiR's real headless engine exposed as tools (src/engine + src/server.js, stdio + HTTP),
> for agents that need live results; needs vite-node/RAM. Path A is the product; B is optional.

## Start here

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): orientation, the two paths (static emission vs MCP engine), the 9 tools, the must-know rules, and a routing table.
- [app/prompts/tool-rules.md](app/prompts/tool-rules.md): SINGLE SOURCE of the tool-argument rules (flat args, name refs, generate-don't-type, fixed-period). Loaded into the MCP model prompt; the Worker restates the same rules against its JSON-draft contract in worker/draftPrompt.js.

## Docs

- [worker/README.md](worker/README.md): the NL→session Cloudflare Worker (path A, the product) — routes, bring-your-own-model, deploy steps, gotchas.
- [docs/tool-contract.md](docs/tool-contract.md): how to generate valid session-building MCP tool calls (path B) — tool signatures, return shapes, plot inputs, a worked example, dynamic-output and failure behaviour.
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md): file map, data flow, engine invariants/gotchas (core singleton, registry-at-startup, discardTp), and how to add a node.
- [docs/app-backend.md](docs/app-backend.md): the LEGACY engine-driven Express NL→session API (superseded by worker/, kept for reference) — BYO-model resolution, validation + SSRF + rate limit, modules, tests.
- [docs/deploy.md](docs/deploy.md): deploy the product (Pages + Worker, free tier, no VM); the MCP server's VM/RAM requirements only if you host it remotely.

## Reference

- [README.md](README.md): human overview, full CLI, tool table, transports.
- Live capability catalogue: call the `list_capabilities` tool (22 analyses, 9 transforms, 9 plots with exact params) — not duplicated statically because it drifts.
